title: The Archive Speaks
caption: The Willowmere Heritage Hearing convenes at last, and the community hall is packed to the walls with neighbors who have spent weeks building something extraordinary. At the podium, Harriet Bloom — resplendent in her green wool cardigan and wire-rim glasses — lays out the archive she and Chloe meticulously assembled: photographs, letters, church records, and testimonies stretching back to 1947. The heritage officer at the front table finds himself dabbing his eyes before she even finishes. Detective Park gives an enthusiastic thumbs-up from the audience, while Chloe beams with quiet pride, clutching her blue notebook. Rosie waves her hand to make sure everyone knows she helped sort the photographs. Even Gerald, seated slightly apart in his too-tight grey suit, watches with an expression that might just be respect. When Harriet closes the binder with a decisive thump, the room knows: the garden's story has been told, and it cannot be ignored.
location: Willowmere town hall — crowded heritage hearing room
story_notes: Harriet Bloom presents the community archive at the public hearing, moving the heritage officer to tears.
